patches.summary: feat(dice-fm): port eventbrite capacity + discount-performance into dice-fm as capacity + tier-performance
patches.notes: capacity is a faithful port (per-event sold = summed order quantity vs capacity = event totalTicketAllocationQty, non-live states excluded). tier-performance is re-targeted from eventbrite discount-performance, not a literal port: DICE exposes no discount/promo/coupon entity, so the command reports per-price-tier redemptions and each tier's share of total redemptions from the real `tickets` store (returned tickets excluded via the `returns` join). Same analytical intent as the eventbrite original; redemption_rate is a share-of-sales rate rather than a sold/cap rate because DICE has no per-tier allocation cap. This is an API-specific adaptation, not a Printing Press generator gap, so no upstream cli-printing-press issue is filed.

patches.summary: Hand-authored entity-normalization subsystem: `normalize` (+ `stats`, `recommend`) canonicalizes free-text ticket-type and venue names into structured axes in a parallel, re-runnable local store.
patches.reason: The generator emits only spec-driven resource commands; DICE's manually-entered ticket-type and venue strings need canonicalization to power cross-event joins and `revenue summary --by-axis`. None of this is derivable from the GraphQL spec.
patches.notes: Parallel and re-runnable: raw synced data is never modified and resolved mappings stay local. `normalize` is intentionally NOT annotated mcp:read-only (it writes the local store); `normalize stats` is read-only and carries the annotation. `normalize recommend` profiles the store and emits a starter config. Read-only annotation discipline confirmed during the 2026-05-27 MCP audit. API-specific value-add, not a generator gap.
